channel.title: NDTV News Search Records Found 1000
channel.description: NDTV.com provides the latest information from and in-depth coverage of India and the world. Find breaking news, India news, top stories, elections, politics, business, cricket, movies, lifestyle, health, live TV, videos, photos and more.

item.title: 5.5 Magnitude Earthquake Hits Chile
item.description: An earthquake measuring 5.5 magnitude struck near coast of central Chile on Friday, the German Research Centre for Geosciences (GFZ) said.
item.content:encoded: An earthquake measuring 5.5 magnitude struck near coast of central Chile on Friday, the German Research Centre for Geosciences (GFZ) said.

item.title: Brazil Court Rules Jair Bolsonaro To Remain Under House Arrest
item.description: Brazil's Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes has ruled that former Brazilian President Jair Bolsonaro can remain under house arrest due to health concerns, a court decision showed on Friday.
item.content:encoded: Brazil's Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes has ruled that former Brazilian President Jair Bolsonaro can remain under house arrest due to health concerns, a court decision showed on Friday.

item.title: Russia Says It Seized Eastern Ukrainian Stronghold Of Kostyantynivka

media:content.media:description: Russia said that Ukraine's Kostyantynivka has been its under control.

item.description: Russia on Friday said it had captured the eastern Ukrainian city of Kostyantynivka, a key post on the road leading to the last major cities in the Donbas region under Kyiv's control.
item.content:encoded: Russia on Friday said it had captured the eastern Ukrainian city of Kostyantynivka, a key post on the road leading to the last major cities in the Donbas region under Kyiv's control.

item.title: Venezuela Earthquake: Death Count Rises To 2,645, Over 12,600 Injured

media:content.media:description: Thousands more are reported still missing after the June 24 back-to-back quakes.

item.description: The death toll from Venezuela's devastating twin earthquakes has risen to 2,645, with more than 12,600 injured, according to new officia figures released on Friday.
item.content:encoded: The death toll from Venezuela's devastating twin earthquakes has risen to 2,645, with more than 12,600 injured, according to new officia figures released on Friday.

item.title: Heatwave In US Delays 250th Independence Day Celebrations

media:content.media:description: New York City's heat index reached 106 degrees on Thursday.

item.description: A blistering heat wave gripping several states across the East Coast of the US prompted the closure of the Great American State Fair here Friday and forced cancellations of the Independence Day celebrations across the region.
item.content:encoded: A blistering heat wave gripping several states across the East Coast of the US prompted the closure of the Great American State Fair here Friday and forced cancellations of the Independence Day celebrations across the region.

item.title: Deaths Surged 29% In France During Week Of Record Heat, Health Agency Says

media:content.media:description: In Paris, funeral service directors have said they've struggled to find places to store bodies

item.description: The new and still incomplete figures from Public Health France doubled its first preliminary estimate of at least 1,000 additional deaths that it gave last Sunday. That earlier estimate covered just three of the hottest days of extreme, deadly heat.
item.content:encoded: The new and still incomplete figures from Public Health France doubled its first preliminary estimate of at least 1,000 additional deaths that it gave last Sunday. That earlier estimate covered just three of the hottest days of extreme, deadly heat.

item.title: On Usha Vance's "Book-Reading" Podcast, Trump Discusses Past Presidents

media:content.media:description: Trump mused about riding a horse after seeing a picture of Abraham Lincoln riding one.

item.description: As Trump turned through the children's book, he made observations about past presidents, cracked some jokes, and threw in a plug for the extensive ballroom he's building on the White House grounds.
item.content:encoded: As Trump turned through the children's book, he made observations about past presidents, cracked some jokes, and threw in a plug for the extensive ballroom he's building on the White House grounds.

item.title: Trump And Netanyahu Hold Phone Call, Agree To Meet "Soon"

media:content.media:description: Trump has been publicly critical of Netanyahu in recent weeks. (File)

item.description: The conversation comes after the United States and Israel fought alongside each other in their military campaign against Iran.
item.content:encoded: The conversation comes after the United States and Israel fought alongside each other in their military campaign against Iran.

item.title: Trump Tightened H-1B Visa Rules, Then Renewals Hit Record High

media:content.media:description: The rise comes as Trump administration attempts to tighten parts of the H-1B system

item.description: The government had proposed a $100,000 fee on many new H-1B applications filed for workers outside the US, but a federal judge blocked the move in June.
item.content:encoded: The government had proposed a $100,000 fee on many new H-1B applications filed for workers outside the US, but a federal judge blocked the move in June.

item.title: Russia Jails 12 'Neo-Nazi' Group Members For Planning To Kill Media Mogul

media:content.media:description: Russian prosecutors did not give an exact motive for the plot.

item.description: Among the other crimes of which the 12 were accused were attacks on migrants and LGBTQ people, as well as committing robberies and inciting ethnic hatred.
item.content:encoded: Among the other crimes of which the 12 were accused were attacks on migrants and LGBTQ people, as well as committing robberies and inciting ethnic hatred.

item.title: Putin Says Fuel Shortage "Not Critical" As Russia Ramps Up Attack On Ukraine

media:content.media:description: Putin has described the attacks on Russian energy as an effort by Ukraine to distract attention.

item.description: The Russian leader appears to believe his government can keep the fuel crisis from eroding his authority and support for the war he launched
item.content:encoded: The Russian leader appears to believe his government can keep the fuel crisis from eroding his authority and support for the war he launched

item.title: Thousands Dead, Records Broken: All You Need To Know About Europe's Heatwave

media:content.media:description: Temperature records were rewritten across Europe.

item.description: World Weather Attribution, a network of climate scientists, said the heatwave was the "most severe ever recorded" based on a three-day forecast of average peak temperatures over the region studied.
item.content:encoded: World Weather Attribution, a network of climate scientists, said the heatwave was the "most severe ever recorded" based on a three-day forecast of average peak temperatures over the region studied.

item.title: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei Funeral News Updates: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's Funeral Begins In Iran

media:content.media:description: Iran Former Supreme Leader's Khamenei Funeral: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's remains arrived in Tehran today.

item.description: Ali Khamenei's Last Rites News Live: Preparations for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's public funeral are taking place in Tehran, following which his remains will be taken to the Iraqi holy cities of Najaf and Karbala.
item.content:encoded: Ali Khamenei's Last Rites News Live: Preparations for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's public funeral are taking place in Tehran, following which his remains will be taken to the Iraqi holy cities of Najaf and Karbala.

item.title: Europe's Deadly Heatwave: Why The World Is Warming At Different Speeds

media:content.media:description: Global sea surface temperatures have reached record levels for this time of year.

item.description: Global temperatures accelerated through June, with the daily average surface air temperature reaching 16.80 degrees Celsius on June 30, 2026, according to Copernicus' ERA5 dataset.
item.content:encoded: Global temperatures accelerated through June, with the daily average surface air temperature reaching 16.80 degrees Celsius on June 30, 2026, according to Copernicus' ERA5 dataset.

item.title: Global Network Used Chat Groups To Drug, Rape Women In 9 Countries, Busted

media:content.media:description: The investigation has revealed how online chat groups were being used to exchange advice on assaults

item.description: The investigation, involving authorities from seven countries, has revealed how online chat groups were being used to exchange advice on drugging victims, avoiding detection and circulating videos and images of the abuse.
item.content:encoded: The investigation, involving authorities from seven countries, has revealed how online chat groups were being used to exchange advice on drugging victims, avoiding detection and circulating videos and images of the abuse.

item.title: EU Sanctions 6 People Involved In Death Of Putin Critic Alexei Navalny

media:content.media:description: People listed under the sanctions programme are subject to an asset freeze.

item.description: The EU said the people were involved with the development of chemical weapons, particularly epibatidine, that were found in samples taken from the activist's body after his death.
item.content:encoded: The EU said the people were involved with the development of chemical weapons, particularly epibatidine, that were found in samples taken from the activist's body after his death.

item.title: Activists Allowed To Sue Sweden Over Failure To Tackle Climate Change

media:content.media:description: The Aurora group has been trying for years to bring a case in Sweden.

item.description: Activist groups around the world are increasingly looking to the courts to force governments and corporations to reduce greenhouse gas emissions and better protect the environment.
item.content:encoded: Activist groups around the world are increasingly looking to the courts to force governments and corporations to reduce greenhouse gas emissions and better protect the environment.

item.title: Ocean Temperatures In June Surge Past Extreme 2023-24 El Nino Records

media:content.media:description: Hotter oceans fuel stronger cyclones, a more humid atmosphere.

item.description: Right now, the average sea surface temperature is just under 21 degrees Celsius across the world's tropical and temperate oceans.
item.content:encoded: Right now, the average sea surface temperature is just under 21 degrees Celsius across the world's tropical and temperate oceans.

item.title: Male Disguise, Remote-Controlled Bomb, Snake Tattoo: Inside Monaco Bomb Attack

media:content.media:description: Anastasiia Berezovska, has a tattoo of a snake on her arm

item.description: The suspect, Anastasiia Berezovska, has a tattoo of possibly a snake on her right arm from the shoulder to the elbow. It says she was born in Ukraine, has dark hair, and speaks German.
item.content:encoded: The suspect, Anastasiia Berezovska, has a tattoo of possibly a snake on her right arm from the shoulder to the elbow. It says she was born in Ukraine, has dark hair, and speaks German.

item.title: Dog Missing For A Month Found Celebrating With FIFA Fans In Mexico

media:content.media:description: The dog immediately recognised her owner, and the reunion took place after a month of separation.

item.description: Social media users also reacted to it, with one X user saying that they loved this update on Gorda.
item.content:encoded: Social media users also reacted to it, with one X user saying that they loved this update on Gorda.
